metric_trajectory.purpose: Track how the primary test metric for each SD has moved across substrate iterations. Enables predictive hypotheses: given the co-variation of metric value vs substrate state, what should the next experiment produce? The trajectory is more informative than any individual PASS/FAIL because it reveals whether the substrate is the bottleneck or the hypothesis.

fields.primary_metric: Name of the key metric this SD should move
fields.primary_metric_description: What it measures and why it is the right quantity
fields.direction: lower_is_better or higher_is_better -- which direction indicates substrate progress
fields.target: Threshold expression the fully-implemented SD must satisfy
fields.observations: Ordered list of experiments that have produced readings on this metric, annotated with substrate state at the time. Chronological order. Include non-contributory and superseded runs -- the zero-readings and failures are the most informative part of the trajectory.
fields.current_blocker: What is currently preventing the metric from reaching threshold, or null if the SD is implemented and validated
fields.prediction: Explicit forward hypothesis: given the current trajectory slope and substrate state, what value range should the next experiment produce, and under what conditions? If null, no confident prediction is possible yet.

queue.title: E2_harm_s counterfactual perturbation training

failure_record.metric: cf_gap_ratio (CONTRASTIVE/OBSERVATIONAL) = 1.29-1.49x across 3 seeds (threshold: > 2.0). CONTRASTIVE always > OBSERVATIONAL (right direction) but advantage too small.
failure_record.target: cf_gap_ratio > 2.0 in 3/3 seeds AND forward_r2 > 0.9 (C1 already passing: r2=0.997-0.999)

queue.implementation_hint: E2_harm_s is implemented (ARC-033) but training requires counterfactual perturbation signal to avoid bias in causal_sig. Add contrastive counterfactual action pair during E2_harm_s training: for each transition, sample a_cf != a_actual, compute predicted z_harm_s_cf, train E2_harm_s to produce divergent outputs for actual vs counterfactual actions. Validates SD-003 z_harm_s attribution pipeline.

metric_trajectory.primary_metric_description: cf_gap_ratio = mean_cf_gap(CONTRASTIVE) / mean_cf_gap(OBSERVATIONAL). The forward model (E2_harm_s) computes the difference between actual and counterfactual z_harm_s; this ratio measures whether contrastive training amplifies that gap relative to observational-only training. Ratio > 2.0 = contrastive training produces substantially larger attribution signal. Also track forward_r2 (C1) as a prerequisite: E2_harm_s must first learn the transition dynamics before contrastive training advantage is interpretable.
metric_trajectory.target: cf_gap_ratio > 2.0 in 3/3 seeds AND forward_r2 > 0.9 (C1 already passing)

observations.substrate_state: SD-013 contrastive perturbation implemented; E2_harm_s trained with a_cf substitution subset
observations.metric_notes: EXQ-320 ran as dry run (reclassified non_contributory 2026-04-10). No metric reading yet.

observations.substrate_state: SD-013 contrastive perturbation implemented; full run (not dry run); 3 seeds
observations.metric_notes: C1 PASS 3/3: forward_r2=0.997-0.999. ARC-033 confirmed: E2_harm_s forward model learns transition dynamics. C3 PASS 3/3: attribution sign correct (1.0 all seeds). C2 FAIL 0/3: cf_gap_ratio=1.49/1.29/1.40 (threshold 2.0). Direction is correct (CONTRASTIVE > OBSERVATIONAL in all seeds) but advantage is only ~30-50% rather than the required 100% (2x). SD-003 reclassified supports (C1+C3 pass). SD-013 does_not_support (ratio too small). The 1.3-1.5x advantage is real -- contrastive training helps -- but the threshold may be too aggressive, OR interventional_fraction=0.3 is too low. Increasing interventional_fraction to 0.5+ or extending training is the predicted fix.

observations.substrate_state: SD-013 contrastive perturbation; interventional_fraction=0.5 (increased from 0.3)
observations.metric_notes: FAIL 7/10 criteria. does_not_support. Increasing interventional_fraction from 0.3 to 0.5 was not sufficient to push cf_gap_ratio above threshold. Direction still correct but ratio below 2.0. Flags for /diagnose-errors: contradicts EXQ-353 PASS on same session.

observations.substrate_state: ARC-033 + SD-003 + SD-013 combined interventional vs observational test
observations.metric_notes: PASS 17/17 criteria. Interventional training distinguishes from observational. All three claims (ARC-033, SD-003, SD-013) tagged as supports. First full PASS for SD-013. Detailed cf_gap_ratio not available (empty metrics.json). CONTRADICTION with EXQ-330a: different experimental design may explain divergence. Flagged for /diagnose-errors.

metric_trajectory.current_blocker: cf_gap_ratio at 1.29-1.49x vs threshold 2.0. Two hypotheses: (1) interventional_fraction=0.3 too low -- the contrastive signal is diluted by 70% observational steps; increasing to 0.5+ may push ratio above 2.0. (2) The 2.0 threshold may be too strict -- a 40% advantage IS meaningful causal identifiability improvement. Consider whether the threshold should be 1.5x instead. Forward model quality (C1, r2>0.99) and sign correctness (C3) are not blockers.

prediction.expected_value: cf_gap_ratio > 2.0 with interventional_fraction >= 0.5; or reclassify threshold to 1.5x if 1.5-2.0x range demonstrates sufficient practical causal identifiability
prediction.rationale: EXQ-330 shows the direction is correct (CONTRASTIVE > OBSERVATIONAL every seed). The 1.3-1.5x advantage is consistent -- this is a stable signal, not noise. Scholkopf et al. 2021 predicts larger advantage with higher interventional fraction. With interventional_fraction=0.3, 30% of steps provide contrastive signal. At 0.5, the training budget for causal correction doubles, and the ratio should increase proportionally.
prediction.prerequisite_state: Re-run with interventional_fraction=0.5 OR re-evaluate whether threshold should be 1.5x. SD-003 already validated -- SD-013 is a refinement, not a prerequisite.

queue.implementation_note_update: SD-013 implementation confirmed complete 2026-04-10 (E2HarmSConfig interventional training). Promoted from candidate to provisional at governance 2026-04-13.

queue.title: Dedicated z_resource encoder for goal-directed navigation

failure_record.metric: benefit_ratio=0.420 (encoder works, prox_r2=0.908, but navigation does not improve)
benefit_ratio=0.113 (encoder converges, goal_resource_r_enc=0.574, but navigation lift absent)
goal_resource_r: z_world=0.99 vs z_resource=0.39-0.86 across 5 seeds (z_world consistently dominates)
failure_record.target: goal_resource_r_resource > goal_resource_r_world (z_resource should align z_goal better than z_world)

queue.implementation_hint: Encoder signal is present (prox_r2=0.9+) but E3 trajectory scoring does not convert encoder output into navigation lift. The bottleneck is action-selection integration: z_resource must feed into E3 trajectory scoring as a goal-proximity gradient, not just be encoded. MECH-112 must be resolved first (E3 structured latent goal representation). MECH-112 prerequisite resolved by co-implementation in this session (ResourceEncoder IS the MECH-112 substrate).

metric_trajectory.primary_metric_description: Cosine similarity between z_goal and z_resource encoder output. Higher = z_goal is seeded from resource-specific features. The staircase pattern here is different from the harm stream: each iteration reveals a new type of zero, not a smooth metric improvement. The zeros are qualitatively distinct failure modes, each requiring a different fix.
metric_trajectory.target: goal_resource_r > 0.2 in RESOURCE_SEED condition vs < 0.1 in WORLD_SEED condition, with n_cosine_samples > 100

observations.substrate_state: z_goal seeded from z_world at resource contact; no dedicated encoder
observations.metric_notes: goal_resource_r stuck at 0.066 across all 085x iterations. Substrate ceiling: z_world at contact encodes full scene (agent position, hazards, resource positions) not object identity. Resources respawn at random locations so z_world at contact has no predictive value for future resource locations. Zero type A: information source conflation.

observations.substrate_state: Proximity regression ResourceEncoder implemented; z_goal seeded from z_resource
observations.metric_notes: prox_r2=0.908 -- encoder works and captures proximity structure. But benefit_ratio=0.42 -- navigation does not improve. Zero type B: encoder captures spatial proximity (same scene structure as z_world) rather than object-identity features. z_resource still scene-grounded.

observations.substrate_state: Updated ResourceEncoder with z_resource seeding; RESOURCE_SEED vs WORLD_SEED discriminative pair
observations.metric_notes: n_cosine_samples=0 in both conditions -- measurement infrastructure never fires. goal_resource_r is undefined (no samples). Zero type C: measurement gap, not a scientific failure. The conditions were never co-activated to allow the cosine comparison to run. Needs /diagnose-errors.

observations.substrate_state: Second concurrent run (same experiment type, same implementation, same seeds)
observations.metric_notes: n_cosine_samples=0 again in all 5 seeds, both conditions. Confirms the bug is reproducible (not a race condition or transient failure). The measurement path never fires regardless of seed or run. Zero type C confirmed persistent.

observations.substrate_state: SD-015 ResourceEncoder implemented; DRIVE_ACTIVE (drive_weight=2.0) vs DRIVE_ABLATED; z_goal seeded from z_resource; 8x8 grid, 3 resources, 150 P0 + 100 P1 episodes
observations.metric_notes: non_contributory (governance-2026-04-12). z_goal_norm=0.0 in 2/3 seeds: seed 42 (0 contact events -- agent never found resources in 250 eps); seed 7 (20 contact events but z_goal_norm=0.0 exactly at all offsets t=0,10,25,50). Seed 13: z_goal_norm=0.128 constant across all offsets (same at t=0 and t=50), suspicious of EMA initialization artifact. DRIVE_ACTIVE vs DRIVE_ABLATED nearly indistinguishable (0.128 vs 0.108). Zero type D: seeding pathway not firing despite resource contacts -- distinct from type C (cosine measurement gap). The seeding event either does not trigger, or z_goal update magnitude is below observable threshold. 150-episode warmup may be insufficient for ResourceEncoder to produce discriminative z_resource.

observations.substrate_state: SD-015 ResourceEncoder wiring FIXED (use_resource_encoder set directly on cfg.latent). Correct pathway active for first time.
observations.metric_notes: PASS 3/3 seeds. wanting_l1_fraction=0.96/0.935/0.985 vs liking_l1_fraction=0.43/0.54/0.43. Dissociation delta=0.53/0.40/0.55. This is behavioral validation of the SD-015 ResourceEncoder pathway. EXQ-074f/234 used z_world fallback; EXQ-354 is the first PASS with the intended SD-015 pipeline. Metric tracked here is wanting_l1_fraction (behavioral proxy) rather than goal_resource_r (cosine) since the wanting/liking pair is the behavioral validation target for MECH-112 + SD-015 integration.

observations.substrate_state: SD-015 wiring correct (use_resource_encoder set directly on cfg.latent). Wanting gradient navigation with ResourceEncoder properly active.
observations.metric_notes: PASS 2/3 seeds (c3_ratios 1.09, 1.43, 704x). Wanting pathway navigation works with correct SD-015 wiring. First PASS for SD-015 behavioral integration. Confirms EXQ-354 pattern with navigation context. Supersedes EXQ-326 (wiring bug).

observations.substrate_state: SD-015 wiring correct. Discriminative pair: RESOURCE_SEED (use_resource_encoder=True) vs WORLD_SEED (use_resource_encoder=False). 5 seeds.
observations.metric_notes: FAIL 0/5 seeds. z_world OUTPERFORMS z_resource for goal alignment (cosine r: 0.99 vs 0.39-0.86). z_resource encoder is present and learns features but z_world's richer scene representation dominates for goal seeding. Zero type E: relative ordering inverted. The encoder IS active and produces signal, but the claim that z_resource should be the BETTER seed is not supported. SD-015 design may need rethinking: perhaps z_resource supplements z_world rather than replacing it.

metric_trajectory.current_blocker: EXQ-322a (2026-04-14) shows z_world dominates z_resource for goal alignment (0.99 vs 0.39-0.86). The encoder is implemented but inverted: z_world is the better seed. SD-015 claim needs reassessment -- z_resource may need to supplement rather than supplant z_world for goal seeding.

prediction.expected_value: goal_resource_r > 0.2 in RESOURCE_SEED, < 0.05 in WORLD_SEED, once measurement gap is fixed
prediction.rationale: EXQ-085l showed the encoder CAN produce signal (prox_r2=0.9+). The current encoder version targets object-type features rather than spatial proximity, so the representational basis is correct. Once the cosine sampling path fires, the RESOURCE_SEED condition should produce measurable cosine similarity since z_resource is now seeding z_goal directly.
prediction.prerequisite_state: /diagnose-errors on EXQ-322 to fix n_cosine_samples=0; then committed-sequence generation must be verified before the downstream gate experiments (MECH-057b, ARC-028) become legible

queue.title: Affective harm non-redundancy constraint
queue.implementation_hint: z_harm_a and z_harm_s must encode non-overlapping information under the split SD-011 design. SD-019 adds an orthogonality/redundancy-reduction constraint (e.g. adversarial loss or correlation penalty) between z_harm_a and z_harm_s encoder outputs. Requires ARC-016 (dynamic precision) to contextualize what counts as non-redundant in different regime states. ARC-016 wiring resolved: e3.current_precision accessible as property.

metric_trajectory.primary_metric_description: cosine_sq_baseline - cosine_sq_nonredundant: the reduction in squared cosine similarity between z_harm_s and z_harm_a produced by the nonredundancy training objective. Positive = objective reduces overlap. Should be positive and consistent across seeds. Also track cosine_sq_nonredundant absolute value (should be < 0.5).
metric_trajectory.target: cosine_sq_nonredundant < cosine_sq_baseline in 3+ seeds AND cosine_sq_nonredundant < 0.5

observations.substrate_state: EMA harm_obs_a (50-dim proximity field); no SD-019 nonredundancy loss; no SD-022 limb damage
observations.metric_notes: r2_s_to_a = 0.996 (EXQ-241b confirmed). Both streams derive from the same proximity field. Nonredundancy objective has no principled basis to separate: the information sources are collinear. This is the pre-SD-019 ceiling state.

observations.substrate_state: SD-019 nonredundancy loss implemented; SD-022 limb damage NOT active (harm_obs_a still from EMA proximity)
observations.metric_notes: C1 (cosine_sq_nonredundant < cosine_sq_baseline) passes 2/5 seeds; direction wrong in 3/5 seeds. C2 (abs < 0.5) passes all 5 seeds -- both streams have low absolute cosine regardless of condition. z_harm_s_norm >> z_harm_a_norm (8.3 vs 0.48 in seed 42): z_harm_a under-expressed in EMA substrate.

observations.substrate_state: Concurrent run on second machine; same seeds, same SD-022-absent substrate
observations.metric_notes: Near bit-for-bit identical to 184158Z (same seeds, near-identical cosine values: 0.11914434 vs 0.11914417). 2/5 seeds pass C1. Confirms the collinear EMA substrate is the bottleneck, not noise or a specific machine/run artifact. The pattern is deterministic.

observations.substrate_state: SD-019 nonredundancy loss active; SD-022 limb_damage_enabled=True (body damage substrate)
observations.metric_notes: PASS all 5 seeds. z_harm_a representationally distinct from z_harm_s; D3 reversal absent. Nonredundancy criterion met. Co-tested with SD-011 and SD-022 -- all three supports. First PASS for SD-019 on production substrate. Prediction confirmed: SD-022 substrate was the rate-limiting factor.

metric_trajectory.current_blocker: SD-022 substrate not active during EXQ-323. When z_harm_a derives from the same 50-dim proximity EMA as z_harm_s, the nonredundancy objective is applied to a pair of representations that share their information source. The training signal is therefore unstable: any reduction in cosine similarity achieved in one episode can be reversed when the proximity geometry changes. SD-022 (limb_damage_enabled=True) provides a causally independent information source for z_harm_a (body damage state), giving the nonredundancy objective a genuine basis for stable disentanglement.

prediction.expected_value: C1 passes 4+/5 seeds; cosine_sq_nonredundant < cosine_sq_baseline consistently; delta > 0.01
prediction.rationale: With SD-022 active, z_harm_a information source (body damage) is causally independent from z_harm_s information source (proximity field). The nonredundancy objective now works with genuinely separable representations. The co-variation across EXQ-241, EXQ-318, and EXQ-323 predicts that source independence is the rate-limiting factor for SD-019 consistency.
prediction.prerequisite_state: SD-022 limb_damage_enabled=True with harm_obs_a_variance > 0.001 (requires adequate hazard density or reduced heal_rate)

queue.title: z_harm_a encodes affective surprise (precision-weighted PE)

failure_record.metric: non_contributory: current z_harm_a trains as EMA accumulator not PE-weighted signal; SD-020 design not yet implemented
failure_record.target: z_harm_a must encode precision-weighted PE (surprise magnitude), not raw accumulated harm; stream distinctness r2_s_to_a < 0.5

queue.implementation_hint: Replace z_harm_a AffectiveHarmEncoder training target from EMA-accumulated harm to a precision-weighted prediction error signal: surprise = |harm_obs - E_predicted_harm| * precision. ARC-016 dynamic precision provides the precision weight. Requires SD-019 to enforce that z_harm_s and z_harm_a encode non-redundant aspects of the harm signal. Both SD-019 and ARC-016 now implemented.

metric_trajectory.primary_metric_description: surprise_corr_pe - surprise_corr_ema per seed: the advantage of PE-target training over EMA-target in encoding harm surprise (correlation between z_harm_a and harm prediction error magnitude). Positive = PE target is better. Also track n_steps (harm events per seed) as a data quality proxy -- below ~50 events, correlations are unreliable.
metric_trajectory.target: surprise_corr_pe > surprise_corr_ema in 3+ seeds AND abs(surprise_corr_pe) > 0.15 AND n_steps >= 50

observations.substrate_state: Before SD-020 implementation: z_harm_a trained as EMA accumulator; no PE target
observations.metric_notes: non_contributory: PE training target not yet implemented. Baseline state.

observations.substrate_state: SD-020 PE target implemented; SD-022 limb damage NOT active; EMA substrate for z_harm_a
observations.metric_notes: 1/5 seeds pass. n_steps=10-20 harm events per seed -- critically low. surprise_corr sign-flips across seeds. harm_obs_a_variance=0.0003-0.0007. Two confounds.

observations.substrate_state: SD-020 PE target; SD-022 NOT active; EMA substrate (second run confirming first)
observations.metric_notes: 1/5 seeds pass again (seed 45 only). Same confounds confirmed: n_steps=10-20, sign-flip across seeds. C1 (surprise_corr_pe > surprise_corr_ema) passes 4/5 seeds -- the direction is right -- but C2 (abs threshold > 0.15) fails in 4/5 seeds due to near-zero variance. The trajectory is stable: two runs on the same substrate produce the same pattern. Both classified mixed. The next fix is the substrate (SD-022), not the implementation.

observations.substrate_state: SD-020 PE target + SD-022 limb_damage_enabled=True
observations.metric_notes: FAIL (reclassified inconclusive on manifest). 2/5 seeds pass with positive signal (mean_surprise_corr_pe=0.12). n_eval_steps=6-10 per seed still too small. Direction positive but n too small for stable correlation. Inconclusive, not does_not_support. Next: increase eval episodes for >= 100 harm events per seed on SD-022 substrate.

metric_trajectory.current_blocker: Two compounding factors confirmed across two runs: (1) n_steps=10-20 events per seed (need >= 50). (2) z_harm_a variance 0.0003-0.0007 on EMA substrate (need > 0.001). SD-022 substrate required. Second run 050533Z confirms the pattern is deterministic and substrate-driven, not run-specific.

prediction.expected_value: 3+/5 seeds show positive surprise_corr_pe - surprise_corr_ema; mean delta > 0.05; abs(surprise_corr_pe) > 0.15
prediction.rationale: On SD-022 substrate, z_harm_a encodes body damage state (slowly-healing, persistent). This makes harm_obs_a_variance > 0.01 plausibly achievable. With that variance floor, the PE target has encoding capacity. Chen (2023) AIC result and Seymour (2019) precision-weighted pain both predict PE > EMA when the encoding channel is not saturated. The co-variation across EXQ-260 -> EXQ-324 -> [next] shows the metric moving in the right direction but gated by information-source variance.
prediction.prerequisite_state: SD-022 limb_damage_enabled=True; eval with >= 100 harm events per seed (increase hazard density, eval_episodes, or reduce heal_rate); SD-019 active to prevent z_harm_a from collapsing to a z_harm_s copy

queue.title: Descending pain modulation: commitment-gated z_harm_s PE suppression

failure_record.metric: non_contributory: SD-021 requires commitment-gated precision modulation of z_harm_s; ARC-016 and MECH-090 not fully implemented
failure_record.target: z_harm_s PE suppressed during committed trajectories (endogenous analgesia analog); measurable as lower z_harm_s_norm during COMMITTED vs UNCOMMITTED episodes under matched harm exposure

queue.implementation_hint: During E3 committed-mode episodes (MECH-090 beta elevated), descending modulation gates z_harm_s PE downward. MECH-090 bistable latch and ARC-016 wiring now both implemented. SD-020 also implemented (precision-weighted PE target for z_harm_s training).

metric_trajectory.primary_metric_description: Ratio of mean z_harm_s_norm during COMMITTED episodes vs UNCOMMITTED episodes, matched for harm proximity. Ratio < 1 = descending modulation suppresses sensory harm encoding during commitment. Requires committed sequences to be generated (total_committed_steps > 0) before the ratio can be computed.
metric_trajectory.target: z_harm_s_norm ratio (committed/uncommitted) < 0.7 in MODULATION_ON vs no effect in MODULATION_OFF, across 2+ seeds

observations.substrate_state: SD-021 design exists; ARC-016 and MECH-090 not fully wired
observations.metric_notes: non_contributory: SD-021 modulation pathway not exercised. No committed sequences generated. The same total_committed_steps=0 blocker that stops EXQ-321 (MECH-090) also stops this experiment.

observations.substrate_state: SD-021 fully wired (ARC-016 + MECH-090 implemented); MODULATION_ON vs MODULATION_OFF conditions
observations.metric_notes: non_contributory (governance-2026-04-11b). n_committed_steps=0 across all 5 seeds in both conditions. Beta_gate elevation never fires -- DESCENDING=CONTROL identically because the attenuation pathway is never reached. Same total_committed_steps=0 blocker confirmed in EXQ-321 (MECH-090) and EXQ-261 (prior SD-021 run). The wiring is present; the training curriculum does not reliably produce committed-mode sequences. Committed sequence generation is the rate-limiting prerequisite.

metric_trajectory.current_blocker: SD-021 depends on MECH-090 committed-mode being active during evaluation (total_committed_steps > 0). EXQ-321 confirmed the same blocker: 5 seeds, all conditions, total_committed_steps=0. Committed sequence generation is the rate-limiting substrate gap for both MECH-090 and SD-021. See MECH-090 staircase: EXQ-049 (gate bypassed) -> EXQ-059b (routing fixed, variance=0) -> EXQ-139 (committed_seq_len=0) -> EXQ-321 (committed_steps=0). The blocker is training curriculum, not wiring.

prediction.expected_value: z_harm_s_norm ratio < 0.7 in MODULATION_ON once committed sequences are reliably generated
prediction.rationale: When MECH-090 bistable gate is exercised (committed_steps > 0), the descending modulation pathway is wired and should produce PE suppression. The biological analog (descending PAG-RVM modulation, Basbaum & Fields) is well-established. The question is implementation fidelity, not mechanism plausibility. Once committed sequences are generated, this should be a relatively clean PASS -- the gating mechanism is implemented, just never exercised.
prediction.prerequisite_state: Training curriculum change that reliably elicits committed-mode sequences (total_committed_steps > 100 per eval episode); MECH-090 bistable latch confirmed working on same curriculum

queue.title: Directional limb damage: body extension for genuine z_harm_s / z_harm_a causal independence

failure_record.metric: r2_s_to_a = 0.996 regardless of harm_history extension; info_gain=14k-22k (substrate ceiling: both streams derive from same proximity field)
ENABLED mean_r2=0.365 vs DISABLED mean_r2=0.702 (r2 reduced by 0.337); but harm_obs_a_variance=0.0004 < threshold 0.001

queue.implementation_hint: IMPLEMENTED 2026-04-09. CausalGridWorldV2 now has 4-directional limb_damage[4] state; accumulates when moving through hazards; heals at heal_rate=0.002/step; harm_obs_a re-sourced from body damage state (7 dims: damage[4]+max+mean+residual_pain) when limb_damage_enabled=True. EXQ-319 PASS confirmed dissociation: agent with accumulated damage shows high z_harm_a and low z_harm_s when removed from hazard. r2 reduction confirmed by EXQ-318 direction (0.702->0.365) though variance threshold unmet. Promote to provisional: mechanism works, threshold failure is calibration (heal_rate too fast for 150 warmup episodes).

metric_trajectory.primary_metric_description: R-squared of z_harm_a predictable from z_harm_s (regression). Value near 1.0 = streams redundant (same information source). Value near 0 = streams carry independent information. The ENABLED vs DISABLED comparison within EXQ-318 is the key: we want a large drop when limb damage is turned on. EXQ-319 uses a complementary paradigm (direct dissociation score) rather than r2.
metric_trajectory.target: r2_s_to_a < 0.5 in LIMB_DAMAGE_ENABLED condition AND harm_obs_a_variance > 0.001 AND dissociation_score > 0.2 (z_harm_a persists when agent leaves hazard)

observations.substrate_state: EMA harm_obs_a (50-dim proximity); no independent body state
observations.metric_notes: r2_s_to_a = 0.996. Substrate ceiling: harm_obs_a is a time-lagged version of the same proximity signal as harm_obs_s. No amount of training-objective manipulation can separate collinear information sources.

observations.substrate_state: Extended harm_history input; still no independent body state
observations.metric_notes: Diagnostic confirmed: harm_history extension does not help. info_gain=14k-22k bits confirms information-source redundancy is the bottleneck. Longer temporal integration of the same signal cannot produce causal independence.

observations.substrate_state: SD-022 ENABLED (limb_damage=True); heal_rate=0.002; 150 warmup eps
observations.metric_notes: ENABLED mean_r2=0.365 vs DISABLED mean_r2=0.702. r2 drops by 0.337 when limb damage is on -- the substrate change produces the predicted direction. FAIL on harm_obs_a_variance < 0.001 (observed: 0.0004): with heal_rate=0.002 and 150 warmup episodes the agent does not accumulate enough damage to generate variance. This is a calibration issue, not a mechanism failure.

observations.substrate_state: SD-022 ENABLED; HIGH_DAMAGE vs FRESH agent dissociation paradigm (different from EXQ-318)
observations.metric_notes: PASS 3/3 seeds. dissociation_score (seed 42)=0.424: z_harm_a_retention_high=0.910 vs z_harm_a_retention_fresh=0.486. z_harm_s drops sharply when agent leaves hazard (z_harm_s_drop=14.06) while z_harm_a persists (ratio 0.91). Convergent evidence from orthogonal paradigm: the body-state z_harm_a IS causally independent of z_harm_s under SD-022 substrate.

observations.substrate_state: SD-022 limb_damage_enabled=True as co-tested substrate in SD-019 nonredundancy experiment
observations.metric_notes: PASS all 5 seeds (co-test). SD-022 substrate validated for production use. SD-019 nonredundancy criterion met. This is the downstream validation prediction from EXQ-319: the body-damage substrate is production-ready, unblocking SD-019 and SD-020 validation experiments.

prediction.expected_value: r2_s_to_a < 0.4 AND harm_obs_a_variance > 0.001 with heal_rate reduced to 0.001 or hazard_density increased
prediction.rationale: EXQ-318 showed the mechanism works (0.37 mean r2 with ENABLED). The variance threshold failure is explained by calibration: with heal_rate=0.002 and 150 warmup episodes, damage doesn't accumulate enough. Reducing heal_rate to 0.001 or doubling eval episodes should cross the variance threshold. EXQ-319 dissociation PASS confirms the qualitative mechanism is sound. Both SD-019 and SD-020 should produce better results on this substrate -- see their metric_trajectory predictions.
